docs: https://dscope.github.io/docs/ (canonical reference; https://app.mydatascope.com/api/external/docs/ is an authenticated in-app mirror of the same surface)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, re-confirmed against the live docs site (https://dscope.github.io/docs/, fetched 2026-07-04): every documented DataScope external-API endpoint (Answers, Locations, Lists/Metadata Objects, Task Assigns, Notifications, Files, Webhooks, Tickets/Findings) is enumerated below. 7 streams (locations/answers/lists/notifications/task_assigns/findings/files) now cover every documented GET list endpoint; 9 writes (location create/update, task assignment, metadata object/type create/update, bulk metadata update, form-answer correction) cover every dialect-expressible mutation. Webhook registration has no programmatic endpoint at all (UI-only configuration, see below) so there is nothing to declare a stream/write for.

excluded.reason: legacy v1 answers listing with a higher 600-record limit and additional metadata columns, but the same underlying record shape as /v2/answers (which this bundle already reads, matching legacy's own choice of the v2 endpoint); v1 and v2 are two views onto the same submitted-answer data, not independently syncable resources
single-list-element detail (by metadata_type + metadata_id) is a strict subset of the lists stream's already-read per-element fields; the lists stream reads every element of every list
single-task-assignment detail is a strict subset of the task_assigns stream's already-read per-record fields
single-ticket detail is a strict subset of the findings stream's already-read per-record fields (Firestore document id is the same identifier used to select the individual record from the list)

webhook subscription registration has no programmatic REST endpoint at all in DataScope's own docs — it is configured exclusively through the mydatascope.com web UI at /webhooks/new; there is no request/response API shape here to declare a write action against
